content: after 2 losses I recommend closing the game. For your own sanity
or not. the sooner you get burnt out in this mode the sooner you will be freed from the chains of traphase
I did not expect that..
I just dont get it man
am I drafting poorly
I feel like pickman is a terrible unit to take and same with gurpy
Pickman is top tier in draft.
Always take if available.

Pickman is pretty much Jenkin without transforming your hand, and he had more synergy with sacrifice and debuffs.

GMurphy is not even terrible, she gives a devastating debuff and paired with Pickman, you would probably receive an average of 96 (2 AOE cards) + 36+ from para and poison cards, assuming he has 2 amp on Pickman, this results to 136+ dmg and an additional 68+ from sacrifice, and pair that with horla and the other things he has, you'll most likely lose
It would deal more but you get the point
All you have is a Jenkin with almost no synergy from any wheel
And looking at the dmg dealt, Aigis dealt more..

Need to get a good carry + damage wheel combo to have a chance of winning.
Your opponent got handed Pickman + We Will Meet Again for +2 Amp on their exalt turn.
You drafted no damage buffs for your Jenkins.
Yup, and horla just cleanses everything
And paired with castor, GMurphy, and all the amp buffs he has, the synergy is just too deadly for you to survive
thank u
out of the frying pan and into the fire
Hameln is back to being the problem child<:Helot_slack_off:1459092159465521184>

do you just hope for a high roll to snipe down a key member, otherwise just hope they low roll so you can get to your wincon sooner?
It doesn't if you finish them before you suffer in the late game
you make a thread in the official feedback channel asking to nerf helot
Pickman is nuts wym

Any time I’m offered Horla, Erica, and a paralyzer yellow, I go for it. Pickman, Jenkin, Tulu. Especially if 24 is on the board. It’s a cheap way to get more damage out of those characters since paralysis doubles and eke out an aliemus lead on account of the lost strikes.
Pickman doesn’t wipe entire teams by turn 3 with his exalt but that doesn’t mean he’s bad for draft that just means Helot is bullshit and shouldn’t exist
are these worth grabbing?
If you want to
Theyre really expensive
probably not
maybe like E3 Special Training if you have a severe lack of DPS wheels but even then probably not

Traphase Wheels are different from normal wheels
Theyre not interchangeable

nothing we can do about it. Thats why I dont play pvp anymore and focus on pve now

Btw, Mochi + Candlestick wheel combo is quite nasty against Helot but gets countered by Hameln<:takenotes:1357186646294138890>
A bit
Hameln 3 turns vs helot 3 turns
Lovely traphase
Who gets their wheel / burst up first.
I was expecting a slower traphase run  after the helot "nerf", so I got myself busy with a poison comp only to be brutally humiliated by a Hameln getting his burst up  5-7  times.

the fact they actually buffed arachne further is extremely funny to me, in my experience playing the hit draft pick mode arachne has a 100% winrate for whichever player gets her <:Doll_Inferno_ha:1459091434958360760>
i balance that winrate with my losses with her
close match against a kath that kept topdecking hyperflare
I thought I was losing for sure
Kath is the new lotan
How does fainted resistance work in traphase?
1st stun / petrify by an enemy's ability will actually stun / petrify you
2nd one on the same unit won't stun / petrify anymore but will x2 the damage
And 3rd can stun / petrify again
Ohhhh now it makes sense
Thanks so much!
There is a stack below the person that got stunned or petrified
Signifying it
The stacks means the enemy won't get petrified or stunned anymore
When hit by the same ability it will remove the stack
Which is where the x2 dmg come from
I see
I was always confused
